add_location_welcome_subtitle: Welcome! Let's set up a new location to organize users, manage access, and connect gateways for activity tracking and monitoring.
add_location_step_common_description: Manage core details and connection parameters for your VPN location.
add_location_step_mfa_description: Configure multi-factor authentication (MFA) to add a secondary verification step to user authentication.
add_location_step_service_location_setti: A special kind of location that allows establishing automatic VPN connections on system boot. Service locations are currently only supported with Defguard Client for Windows.
add_location_step_access_control_descrip: Assign user groups with access permissions to this location.
add_location_step_firewall_description: The default policy defines how to handle traffic not covered by ACL rules.
add_location_firewall_no_license_subtitl: Firewall is available only in Business and Enterprise plans. You can still create location now and activate Firewall later by upgrading your plan.
add_location_service_prelogon_content: The VPN connects at system boot and disconnects after user login, useful for one-time authentication with external identity providers like Active Directory.
add_location_service_always_on_content: Always-on: the VPN connects at system boot and stays active until the network, client, or mode changes. Use when constant access is needed.
add_location_firewall_gateway_activation: Activate location by setting up gateway
To activate your location, you must add at least one gateway connection. You can do this immediately after creation or later in the location settings.
Run the gateway activation wizard once the location is created
add_location_start_public_address_descri: Used by VPN users to connect.
add_location_start_label_endpoint: Gateway VPN IP address or domain name
add_location_internal_vpn_gateway_addres: The VPN network will be derived from this address (e.g., 10.10.10.1 → 10.10.10.0). You can specify multiple addresses separated by commas. The first one is used as the primary address for device IP assignment.
add_location_internal_vpn_label_address: Gateway VPN IP address and netmask
add_location_internal_vpn_allowed_ips_de: List of addresses/masks that should be routed through the VPN network.
add_location_internal_vpn_allowed_ips_fr: Allowed IPs are generated automatically from firewall rules
add_location_mfa_internal_content: Uses the MFA methods configured in your Defguard profile.
add_location_mfa_external_content: Requires configuring an external identity provider in the settings, such as Google, Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, or JumpCloud.
location_firewall_option_default_allow: Users/devices can access all resources unless limited by ACL rules.
location_firewall_option_default_deny: All traffic not explicitly allowed by an ACL rule will be blocked.
location_form_label_endpoint: Gateway IP address or domain name
location_mfa_service_location_warning: MFA can't be enabled for service locations. To enforce MFA, select the Regular location type.
location_service_mode_mfa_warning: MFA-protected locations can't be set as service locations. Disable MFA to use a service location type.
network_device_add_error: Failed to load network data for adding device
network_devices_empty_title: You don't have any network devices.
network_devices_empty_subtitle: To add one, click the button below.
location_edit_addresses_rewrite_warning: Changing the Gateway VPN IP address or netmask will automatically reassign any device addresses that fall outside the new network range to a randomly selected available IP address.
location_edit_service_location_mfa_warni: Service locations can't use MFA. To enforce MFA, select the "Regular location" type.
location_edit_mfa_service_location_warni: MFA-protected locations can't be set as service locations. Disable MFA to use service location mode. You can read more about service locations in our documentation.
locations_empty_subtitle: Click the button below to add one.
locations_overview_empty_subtitle: Connect to any location to view summary statistics.
location_overview_empty_title: This location doesn't have any data.
location_overview_empty_subtitle: Data for this location will appear once it is connected.
location_overview_connected_users_empty_: Wait for a user to connect
location_overview_connected_network_devi: Wait for a device to connect
